sanskrit: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च। निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी।। सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः। मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः।।
transliteration: adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṃ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca | nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣamī || santuṣṭaḥ satataṃ yogī yatātmā dṛḍha-niścayaḥ | mayy arpita-mano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Not envious toward any being, friendly and compassionate, without sense of ownership or false ego, equipoised in pain and pleasure, forgiving, ever-content, steadfast in yoga, self-controlled, with mind and intellect dedicated to Me—such a devotee is dear to Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna outlines the psychological profile of a person whose identity has shifted from egoic attachment to relational compassion and inner stability; it is a map of post-egoic mental health where self-worth is no longer traded for gain, loss, approval, or rejection, allowing unconditional friendliness and calm to become the default affect.
psychological_state: A person who has begun to experience identity beyond possessions and reputation, feeling both tender openness to others and a grounded immunity to life's ups and downs.
trigger_scenario: After a painful divorce stripped away roles of spouse and homeowner, the person now volunteers at an animal shelter, discovering a steady inner quiet that no longer depends on external validation.
anchor_text: Meet everyone with kindness, keep steady through ups and downs, and let go of ego.
